differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL.

safetyChecks.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.
safetyChecks.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
safetyChecks.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

requiredExam.label: Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation

source.source_section: Adrenal Insufficiency curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue. Hyperpigmentation supports primary adrenal insufficiency or ACTH excess and changes ACTH, antibody, renin/aldosterone, or steroid-replacement interpretation.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa, palmar creases, scars, and sun-protected skin for diffuse or patchy hyperpigmentation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When primary adrenal insufficiency, Addison disease, ACTH excess, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Hyperpigmentation supports primary adrenal insufficiency or ACTH excess and changes ACTH, antibody, renin/aldosterone, or steroid-replacement interpretation.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Adrenal Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

source.source_section: Adrenal Insufficiency curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dry mucous membranes change adrenal-crisis severity assessment, fluid urgency, and stress-dose steroid planning.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting, vomiting, or poor intake is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Dry mucous membranes change adrenal-crisis severity assessment, fluid urgency, and stress-dose steroid planning.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Adrenal Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Test chair-rise strength

source.source_section: Adrenal Insufficiency curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment. Clinically important weakness changes crisis severity, fall risk, and monitored-care threshold.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.
requiredExam.technique: Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting illness, or functional weakness is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Clinically important weakness changes crisis severity, fall risk, and monitored-care threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Adrenal Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Palpate abdomen for tenderness

conditionalExam.action: Add when adrenal crisis, vomiting, abdominal pain, shock is present; this evaluates abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness. Tenderness changes adrenal crisis severity and mimic evaluation.

conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the abdomen gently, localizing tenderness and stopping if guarding, rebound, or severe pain occurs.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal crisis, vomiting, or abdominal pain is possible
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness

conditionalExam.limitations: Palpate abdomen for tenderness is supportive for Adrenal Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

source.source_section: Adrenal Insufficiency curated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
initialTests.rationale: Provides diagnostic or safety thresholds that change interpretation and management for Adrenal Insufficiency.
initialTests.diagnostic_target: 8 AM cortisol and ACTH.: objective test, threshold, imaging, or baseline data that confirms diagnosis, identifies complications, or routes care for Adrenal Insufficiency.
initialTests.management_change: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
initialTests.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
initialTests.limitations: Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Adrenal Insufficiency test or threshold.

initialTests.label: Cosyntropin stimulation test.

initialTests.label: Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock.
